title: APR Queue Event (append-only JSONL line)
description: One JSON object per line in .apr/queue/<workflow>.events.jsonl. State for a given entry is derived by folding events in append order. See docs/schemas/queue-events.md for the prose specification.

schema_version.description: Stable identifier; bumped to v2 only on breaking changes.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the event was recorded.

event.description: Event kind. See queue-events.md for state derivation rules.

entry_id.description: Stable unique identifier for the queue entry (ULID/UUID recommended).

round.description: Round number for this entry. Required on `enqueue`.

include_impl.description: Whether to include the implementation document. Recorded at enqueue time.

runner_id.description: Identifier of the runner process that picked up this entry. Required on `start`.

started_at.description: Wall-clock timestamp when the round began. Required on `start`.

finished_at.description: Wall-clock timestamp when the round reached a terminal state. Required on `finish` / `fail` / `cancel`.

ok.description: Required on `finish` and `fail`. Must be true on `finish` and false on `fail`.

code.description: Stable taxonomy code (per bd-3tj). Required on `finish` (`ok`) and `fail`.

exit_code.description: Process exit code of the underlying round. Required on `finish` and `fail`.

output_path.description: Path to the round output markdown. Required on `finish` (success path); null on `fail`/`cancel`.

slug.description: Actual oracle slug used for this run. Required on `finish` and `fail`.

stderr_digest.description: Optional sha256 of the truncated stderr excerpt (32 KB tail). Useful for debugging without committing logs.

canceled_at.description: Wall-clock timestamp when the cancel event was recorded. Required on `cancel`.

reason.description: Free-form reason for cancel/fail (e.g. `operator` for cancel, `busy_timeout` for fail). Never contains secrets.
